identity.canonical_title: Constrained Dipole Configurations and Field Solution: U_constraint

theory.canonical_statement: The magnetic energy density is \[ u_{\mathrm{mag}}(\mathbf{r},t) = \frac{1}{2\mu_0}\,\mathbf{B}(\mathbf{r},t)\cdot\mathbf{B}(\mathbf{r},t)\, \] so that the total magnetic energy inside radius $R$ is \[ U_{\mathrm{mag}} = \int_0^R\int_0^\pi\int_0^{2\pi} \frac{|\mathbf{B}(\mathbf{r},t)|^2}{2\mu_0}\,r^2\sin\theta\,dr\,d\theta\,d\phi\,. \] Mechanical compression of the dipoles also contributes work. If an external force $F_{\mathrm{mech}}$ acts to maintain separation $d$, the \emph{constraint energy} functional can be written \[ U_{\mathrm{constraint}} = U_{\mathrm{mag}} + F_{\mathrm{mech}}\,d\, \] with $F_{\mathrm{mech}} = -\nabla_d U_{\mathrm{interaction}}$. In the point-dipole limit, the force between opposite dipoles is approximately \[ F_{\mathrm{mech}} = -\nabla_d \biggl[-\frac{\mu_0}{4\pi}\frac{(\boldsymbol{\mu}_1\cdot\boldsymbol{\mu}_2) - 3(\boldsymbol{\mu}_1\cdot \hat d)(\boldsymbol{\mu}_2\cdot \hat d)}{d^3}\biggr] = \frac{3\mu_0}{2\pi}\frac{\boldsymbol{\mu}_1\cdot\boldsymbol{\mu}_2 - 2(\boldsymbol{\mu}_1\cdot \hat d)(\boldsymbol{\mu}_2\cdot \hat d)}{d^4}, \] where $\hat d$ is t
theory.technical_interpretation: Constrained Dipole Configurations and Field Solution: U_constraint is an algebraic definition, written U_constraint = U_mag + F_mech d. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from U_, F_, defining U_constraint. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
